primary_accounting_issue: Détermination, pour une licence de propriété intellectuelle relevant d’IFRS 15, de la nature du droit transféré afin de choisir entre les deux modèles de reconnaissance du revenu applicables à la licence

authority_resolution.selection_reason: Only IFRS 15 is provided as a governing standard for revenue from licences of intellectual property, so applying the precedence rule strictly, IFRS 15 is the single governing lineage and no overlapping lower-precedence document remains.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low; the context contains the specific IFRS 15 licence guidance that directly addresses whether licence revenue is recognised over time or at a point in time.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern the two licence models and the criteria for choosing between them.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify that the licence guidance applies when the licence is distinct and otherwise the licence is combined with other promises.
These paragraphs provide the distinct-performance-obligation framework needed before applying the specific licence model in B56.
These paragraphs clarify when a customer reasonably expects activities that significantly affect the intellectual property.
This paragraph constrains the assessment by identifying factors to disregard when distinguishing right to access from right to use.
These paragraphs provide the general over-time versus point-in-time recognition consequences once the licence model has been identified.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address the royalty exception, which is an overlay on measurement and timing rather than a separate peer model for identifying the licence approach.
These paragraphs address measurement of progress after an over-time model has already been selected.
These paragraphs address transaction price and related overlays rather than identifying the peer licence recognition models.

treatment_families.family: Assessment of a distinct licence performance obligation before applying licence-specific revenue recognition guidance
Licence grants access to intellectual property throughout the licence period
Licence grants use of intellectual property as it exists when granted

approaches.label: Right to access the entity's intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 treats this as a distinct licence model when the customer is granted ongoing access to intellectual property affected by the entity's activities.

approaches.label: Right to use the entity's intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 treats this as a distinct licence model when the customer is granted use of intellectual property as it exists at the grant date.
